title: Oracle For Large-Gauge Quantization Before Hall-Response Equivalence
summary: Detailed oracle for the large-gauge quantization prerequisite in the Lecture Two theorem family.
assumptions: The answer must not rely on later Hall-current or TKNN statements to justify integrality.
scope: Detailed answer key for the large-gauge prerequisite proof.

source_anchors.notes: The answer must reconstruct the S^2 times S^1 argument itself.

derivation_spine: Insert one unit of flux through the spatial two-sphere.
Evaluate the Chern-Simons functional on S^2 times S^1 and identify it with the holonomy angle.
Apply the large gauge transformation that shifts the holonomy by 2pi.
Demand invariance of exp(i k I_CS) and conclude that k must be integral.
mandatory_logical_moves: The unit-flux background must be named explicitly.
The 2pi holonomy shift must appear explicitly.
The conclusion k in Z must be derived from path-integral invariance.
common_failure_patterns: Claiming Chern-Simons levels are quantized without the specific background argument.
Using later parts of the Hall theorem to justify the prerequisite.
grading_rubric: Pass requires the actual large-gauge proof.
Unsafe-pass is not allowed here because the question is only about the local prerequisite proof.
Partial is appropriate if the conclusion is known but the holonomy-shift step is missing.
failure_triggers: Fail if the answer only quotes the integer result without the S^2 times S^1 construction.
Fail if the answer invokes the Hall current or Berry curvature before the level is quantized.
